name: Gartner
bullCase: Gartner's enterprise IT research is the most defensible subscription moat in B2B services — Fortune 500 clients renew at 90%+ rates because Gartner's Magic Quadrant dictates vendor selection. Aggressive buybacks (~6% float reduction annually) compound EPS well above organic growth. After a 36% selloff, the stock trades at an anomalously low 11x forward PE.
bearCase: Enterprise IT budgets are among the first to be cut in a downturn. If new contract volumes slow in a recession, Gartner's double-digit growth engine stalls. The buyback program — funded by FCF — is the primary EPS driver; any FCF pressure creates a compounding miss.

cagrModel.basis: Reduced sensitivity to mean-reversion; reflects modest growth profile.

screener.note: Valuation score 0.98 exceeds 0.8 limit (Upgraded from FAIL: Protected by elite quality floor (QCS 10)) (REJECT: Stalled at WAIT with base CAGR 3% < 15%)

description: Gartner, Inc. provides business and technology insights for decisions and performance on an organization's mission-critical priorities in the United States, Canada, Europe, the Middle East, Africa,...
